# Notes on LaTeX

**Updated April, 2026**

## Basic setup
```tex
\DocumentMetadata{
  lang        = en-US,
  pdfstandard = ua-2,
  pdfstandard = a-4f, %or a-4
  tagging=on,
  tagging-setup={math/setup=mathml-SE}
}

\documentclass{article}
\usepackage[utf8]{inputenc}
\usepackage{hyperref}

\hypersetup{
    pdflang=en-US,
    pdftitle={<title of PDF>},
    pdfauthor={<author>}
}
```

### Notes

* The `hypersetup` part sets a title in the PDF's metadata which Brightspace's checker flags if this isn't present.
* Compile with LuaLaTeX
* Use the most up-to-date version of LuaLaTeX available.

## Further reading

* [Overleaf documentation](https://docs.overleaf.com/writing-and-editing/creating-accessible-pdfs)
* based on the [LaTeX Project's documentation](https://latex3.github.io/tagging-project/documentation/usage-instructions)
* [Overleaf's article digging deeper](https://www.overleaf.com/learn/latex/An_introduction_to_tagged_PDF_files%3A_internals_and_the_challenges_of_accessibility)

::: {.callout-note}
Read the Overleaf or LaTeX project for more info on tables, images, document structure, etc.
:::
